title: phaseolus vulgaris var. spadiceus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. spadiceus is a variant of the common bean plant, known for its red or reddish-brown seed pods and is also known as the "red kidney bean". The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the production of lectins, which are toxic compounds found in some legumes. The seeds of this plant have been used to treat various health conditions, including kidney problems and high cholesterol levels, due to their high fiber and protein content, as well as their ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Phaseolus vulgaris var. spadiceus seeds can be found in supplement form, often used to support kidney function and overall well-being.
